proposed_from.topic: Sharp phase-transition theorem for Honest-DiD sensitivity analysis (Rambachan and Roth, 2023, Review of Economic Studies) and its descendants (Roth 2024 NBER). Honest-DiD relaxes the parallel trends assumption by bounding the deviation of the treatment-cohort post-trend from a linear pre-trend extrapolation by an M-budget, and reports a robust confidence interval CI(M) for the dynamic ATT that grows monotonically in M. The flagship question: characterize the sharp threshold M_star at which CI(M) first crosses zero (the smallest pre-trend deviation that the data CANNOT rule out a null treatment effect under), as a closed-form functional of the observable pre-trend covariance matrix and the post-period treatment-effect point estimate. The kernel claim is a sharp three-regime phase transition: when M < M_star_lo the null is robustly rejected for every admissible deviation; when M_star_lo <= M < M_star_hi the null is rejected only on a calibrated subset of admissible deviations parametrized by the projection direction; when M >= M_star_hi the null is robustly irrefutable. The theorem additionally provides closed-form expressions for M_star_lo and M_star_hi as singular values of an explicit observable kernel matrix combining the pre-period sample covariance, the post-period coefficient vector, and the second-difference smoothness operator, generalizing the Rambachan-Roth single-threshold characterization to the three-regime structure. Recovers Rambachan-Roth point identification at M=0 and provides the first sharp robust-inference phase transition with computable thresholds for staggered DiD.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains q1_spectral_phase_transition/p1_markov, which is a row-space phase-transition template with unresolved row-space/box affine-fiber geometry, and q1_partial_did_unobserved_trends/p1_baseline_v2, which treats staggered DiD partial identification under partially unobserved pretrends. The revised proposal differs by targeting the Honest-DiD aggregation quotient: an active-face matrix condition in A, L, L_agg, ell, and Sigma that classifies when aggregate sensitivity is sharp, conservative, or anti-conservative relative to cohort-level trend-deviation budgets.","published_axis":"Closest published results are rambachan2023parallel for fixed-M Honest-DiD sensitivity, liu2025cohortanchored for cohort-period robust inference warning against aggregated event-study sensitivity, and wright2026designrobust for staggered-adoption diagnostics plus sensitivity-robust inference under restricted parallel-trend violations. The revised epsilon-gap is that Conjecture 1 supplies an ex ante primitive active-face equality certificate for aggregate-versus-cohort breakdown frontiers, not a replacement workflow, contamination diagnostic, or fixed-M confidence procedure."}
seed_list: Prior work RambachanRoth2023 would imply that Honest-DiD breakdown values are computed fixed-M sensitivity outputs; our seed conjectures that the first zero crossing has a sharp singular-value phase diagram because covariance whitening turns the smoothness budget into an observable ellipsoid.

Prior work RambachanRoth2023 and AndrewsRothPakes2023 establish valid fixed-M robust inference; our seed conjectures that the controlling face changes only at finitely many symbolic active-set thresholds because the zero-null support function has a deterministic polyhedral face lattice.

Prior work CallawaySantAnna2021, SunAbraham2021, and WingFreedmanHollingsworth2024 would suggest aggregation mainly changes estimands and weights; our seed conjectures that aggregation can change the Honest-DiD breakdown threshold discontinuously through rank loss in the cohort-period bias kernel.

Prior ThmSmith pid_did_anticipation_bounded/v1 says independent anticipation boxes give sharp intervals but coupled restrictions may make them outer bounds; our seed conjectures that Honest-DiD second-difference coupling is exactly the support-function correction that closes this sharpness gap.

Prior work MastenPoirier2020 treats breakdown frontiers as inferential targets; our seed conjectures that the Honest-DiD crossing time itself has nonstandard kink asymptotics because the active singular direction can be weakly binding.

Prior work RothSantAnna2023FunctionalForm shows parallel trends can be functional-form sensitive; our seed conjectures that M_star is invariant only under transformations preserving the whitened second-difference cone, and otherwise is a scale artifact.

Prior work CallawayGoodmanBaconSantAnna2024 shows continuous-treatment DiD dose comparisons can retain selection bias; our seed conjectures that rescaling dose changes the Honest-DiD threshold through a generalized singular value unless the dose map is an isometry of the bias kernel.

Prior work RambachanRoth2023 reports monotone widening of CI(M); our seed conjectures that two studies with identical scalar breakdown values can have opposite robustness interpretations because one crossing is driven by a full-dimensional cap and the other by a nearly null singular direction.

Prior work AndrewsRothPakes2023 delivers valid conditional moment-inequality inference; our seed conjectures that Honest-DiD has reusable tie-case formulas for simultaneous active faces because the nuisance pretrend equality pins the affine fiber to a low-dimensional quotient.

Prior work WingFreedmanHollingsworth2024 shows stacked DiD changes implicit trend weights; our seed conjectures that stacking can reverse the ordering of robustness thresholds across outcomes by rotating the covariance-whitened pretrend-bias singular vectors.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work RambachanRoth2023 would imply that Honest-DiD breakdown values are computed fixed-M sensitivity outputs; our seed conjectures that the first zero crossing has a sharp singular-value phase diagram because covariance whitening turns the smoothness budget into an observable ellipsoid.

anchor_paper.author: Ashesh Rambachan and Jonathan Roth
anchor_paper.claim: They recommend reporting robust confidence sets over M and show empirical breakdown values, but compute them through the sensitivity procedure rather than a symbolic threshold formula.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift, not blocked: the seed adds a sign-changing phase diagram for the zero-crossing geometry, not a routine assumption weakening.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from RambachanRoth2023 because CI(M) is already monotone in M.
seed_details.rebuttal: Monotonicity gives existence of a crossing, not a closed-form singular-value two-threshold active-direction classification.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work RambachanRoth2023 and AndrewsRothPakes2023 establish valid fixed-M robust inference; our seed conjectures that the controlling face changes only at finitely many symbolic active-set thresholds because the zero-null support function has a deterministic polyhedral face lattice.

anchor_paper.author: Isaiah Andrews, Jonathan Roth, and Ariel Pakes
anchor_paper.claim: Linear conditional moment-inequality inference delivers validity with nuisance parameters, leaving symbolic active-set geometry as a separate problem.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/active-face kernel, not blocked: the target is a named published inference workflow and the obstruction/classification is over an open class of covariance and restriction matrices.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from linear programming duality because Honest-DiD restrictions are linear inequalities.
seed_details.rebuttal: LP duality gives a computation, not the symbolic M-intervals, face ties, and zero-excluded/tangent/zero-included classification.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CallawaySantAnna2021, SunAbraham2021, and WingFreedmanHollingsworth2024 would suggest aggregation mainly changes estimands and weights; our seed conjectures that aggregation can change the Honest-DiD breakdown threshold discontinuously through rank loss in the cohort-period bias kernel.

anchor_paper.author: Brantly Callaway and Pedro H. C. Sant'Anna
anchor_paper.claim: Group-time ATT parameters are identified under staggered timing and conditional parallel trends, but sensitivity thresholds for violations are not derived at the aggregation-operator level.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assignment/aggregation variation, not blocked: the novelty is rank loss and threshold discontinuity, not applying an estimator to another setting.
seed_details.referee_objection: This follows from SunAbraham2021 contamination weights because aggregation already changes coefficient interpretation.
seed_details.rebuttal: Contamination weights do not characterize sharp Honest-DiD smoothness thresholds or singular-value rank loss of the bias kernel.

seed_details.one_line: Prior ThmSmith pid_did_anticipation_bounded/v1 says independent anticipation boxes give sharp intervals but coupled restrictions may make them outer bounds; our seed conjectures that Honest-DiD second-difference coupling is exactly the support-function correction that closes this sharpness gap.

anchor_paper.author: ThmSmith prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: If substantive restrictions couple anticipation distortions, the support interval may be conservative.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: this is a coupled-support sharpness phenomenon for a prior-proposal gap, not a paper-to-paper setting extension.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just the support function of a convex set, so it is textbook convex analysis.
seed_details.rebuttal: The support function is textbook; the gap is the exact Honest-DiD coupled second-difference operator and the comparison to the banked independent-box interval.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MastenPoirier2020 treats breakdown frontiers as inferential targets; our seed conjectures that the Honest-DiD crossing time itself has nonstandard kink asymptotics because the active singular direction can be weakly binding.

anchor_paper.author: Matthew A. Masten and Alexandre Poirier
anchor_paper.claim: Breakdown frontiers quantify how much baseline assumptions can be relaxed before a conclusion changes, creating a target distinct from a fixed-assumption confidence set.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation, not blocked: inference for the random crossing time reveals kink/weak-binding behavior hidden by fixed-M coverage.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from MastenPoirier2020 because they already do inference on breakdown frontiers.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their frontier theory is not specialized to Honest-DiD's covariance-whitened event-study crossing with active singular-vector ties.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work RothSantAnna2023FunctionalForm shows parallel trends can be functional-form sensitive; our seed conjectures that M_star is invariant only under transformations preserving the whitened second-difference cone, and otherwise is a scale artifact.

anchor_paper.author: Jonathan Roth and Pedro H. C. Sant'Anna
anchor_paper.claim: Parallel trends is insensitive to functional form only under strong restrictions, so robustness conclusions can depend on the outcome scale.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/invariance phenomenon, not blocked: the surprise is a necessary-and-sufficient invariance condition for M_star, not another functional-form warning.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate because any nonlinear transformation changes event-study coefficients.
seed_details.rebuttal: The question is sharper: which transformations preserve the threshold functional after covariance and smoothness whitening.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CallawayGoodmanBaconSantAnna2024 shows continuous-treatment DiD dose comparisons can retain selection bias; our seed conjectures that rescaling dose changes the Honest-DiD threshold through a generalized singular value unless the dose map is an isometry of the bias kernel.

anchor_paper.author: Brantly Callaway, Andrew Goodman-Bacon, and Pedro H. C. Sant'Anna
anchor_paper.claim: For continuous treatments, interpreting differences across doses is challenging because generalized parallel trends does not rule out selection bias across doses.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation, not blocked: the dose scaling exposes a singular-value invariance failure hidden in scalar M reporting.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a units problem, since M rescales with the treatment dose.
seed_details.rebuttal: The conjecture distinguishes harmless scalar units from non-isometric dose maps that rotate pretrend-bias directions and change threshold ordering.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work RambachanRoth2023 reports monotone widening of CI(M); our seed conjectures that two studies with identical scalar breakdown values can have opposite robustness interpretations because one crossing is driven by a full-dimensional cap and the other by a nearly null singular direction.

anchor_paper.author: Ashesh Rambachan and Jonathan Roth
anchor_paper.claim: The paper reports sensitivity plots and breakdown values, but its formal coverage guarantees are stated for robust confidence sets under specified restrictions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition, not blocked: it decomposes the same scalar breakdown into active-direction geometry with different empirical meaning.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a visualization choice rather than a theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed invariant is the dimension and curvature of the active cap, which is a theorem-level property of the bias kernel.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AndrewsRothPakes2023 delivers valid conditional moment-inequality inference; our seed conjectures that Honest-DiD has reusable tie-case formulas for simultaneous active faces because the nuisance pretrend equality pins the affine fiber to a low-dimensional quotient.

anchor_paper.author: Isaiah Andrews, Jonathan Roth, and Ariel Pakes
anchor_paper.claim: Linear conditional moment-inequality inference delivers validity with nuisance parameters, leaving symbolic active-set geometry as a separate problem.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/tie-case phenomenon, not blocked: it targets symbolic simultaneous-face formulas for a named workflow rather than generic LP validity.
seed_details.referee_objection: Tie cases are measure-zero algebra and not publishable.
seed_details.rebuttal: Breakdown thresholds occur exactly at boundary ties, so the tie algebra governs reported empirical robustness values.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work WingFreedmanHollingsworth2024 shows stacked DiD changes implicit trend weights; our seed conjectures that stacking can reverse the ordering of robustness thresholds across outcomes by rotating the covariance-whitened pretrend-bias singular vectors.

anchor_paper.author: Coady Wing, Seth M. Freedman, and Alex Hollingsworth
anchor_paper.claim: Stacked DiD can put different implicit weights on treatment and control trends, so aggregation can change the target and the robustness interpretation.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sign/rank regime-change, not blocked: the seed predicts threshold-order reversal from a named estimator workflow, not just different weights.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is already implied by stacked DiD using different weights.
seed_details.rebuttal: Different weights do not by themselves imply an SVD threshold-order reversal for Honest-DiD robustness.

proposed_from.literature_map: Rambachan and Roth (2023, REStud) provide the central Honest-DiD framework and report CI(M) breakdown values, but do not give a symbolic zero-crossing threshold. Roth (2022, AER Insights) shows why binary pretrend tests are low-powered, motivating continuous sensitivity frontiers. Manski and Pepper (2018, REStat) and Masten and Poirier (2020, QE) anchor bounded-variation and breakdown-frontier logic. Andrews, Roth, and Pakes (2023, REStud) provide linear conditional moment-inequality inference, leaving active-face symbolic geometry separate. Callaway and Sant'Anna (2021, JoE), Sun and Abraham (2021, JoE), Goodman-Bacon (2021, JoE), and Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess (2024, REStud) define the modern staggered/event-study coefficient frontier. Roth and Sant'Anna (2023, JPE Micro) make covariance-aware staggered estimation salient. Wing, Freedman, and Hollingsworth (2024, NBER) and Liu (2025, arXiv) warn that aggregation changes robustness interpretation. Roth and Sant'Anna (2023, Econometrica) and Callaway, Goodman-Bacon, and Sant'Anna (2024, NBER) show functional-form and treatment-scale choices can change what parallel trends means.

banked_reason: Re-review under widened flagship rubric (axes b/c added) — Stage -0.5 angle 2 v5 verdict flipped from REVISE@flagship (original cap-exhausted run) to REJECT@not-publishable. New rubric is stricter for non-axis-(a/b/c) proposals: Honest-DiD M_star threshold is a calibration parameter (sensitivity intercept), not a non-equivalence between named estimators (b) or a strict extension of a previously-open class (c). Pipeline NO-PASS'd in 178s after 1 reviewer call. Math sound at field; flagship structurally unattainable for sensitivity-threshold framing.
